summary: Prepare PR governance for the React Agent Workbench rebuild stack.
rationale: The approved React Agent Workbench rewrite intentionally replaces the retired frontend with React, adds generated visual/static assets, and needs a dedicated major-refactor goal so child PRs can be governed by explicit manifests instead of bypassing red-zone checks.
verification: uv run ruff check tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run ty check tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run --group dev python tools/check_pr_governance.py --base origin/main
deletion_targets: legacy frontend path assumptions in PR governance
ordinary file-budget treatment for generated React workbench visual and packaged static assets
risks: The React Workbench file budget must remain scoped to its explicit goal_id, not a global relaxation.
Generated asset exclusions must not hide production source changes.
Child PRs must carry their own React Workbench major-refactor manifest instead of relying on this bootstrap manifest.
